perturbation.original_text: USA MCO would receive one third of the monthly subscription price, per month, per subscriber (approximately $6.65). All revenue sharing opportunities will be calculated for all parties after any credit card or other third party processing fees are deducted from the gross sale.
perturbation.changed_text: USA MCO would receive one third of the monthly subscription price, per month, per subscriber (approximately $6.65). All profit sharing opportunities will be calculated *before* any credit card or other third party processing fees are deducted from the gross sale.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'revenue sharing' to 'profit sharing' and altering the calculation order introduces a contradiction. The original text specifies deductions *after* processing fees, while the modified text states deductions *before* processing fees. This discrepancy affects the amount USA MCO receives and creates uncertainty about the actual compensation, and if the agreement is about the revenue or about the profit that is made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IMedicor will charge a subscription fee to participating USA MCO providers. The suggested monthly subscription would be $19.95 per month per subscriber. USA MCO would receive one third of the monthly subscription price, per month, per subscriber (approximately $6.65). All profit sharing opportunities will be calculated *before* any credit card or other third party processing fees are deducted from the gross sale. USA MCO will be responsible for the marketing and initial / ongoing communication to their network highlighting the features of iMedicor, the value proposition, and their support of the project. This will not be a direct sales requirement for USA MCO, but only an agreement to provide access to the USA MCO user base
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'revenue sharing' to 'profit sharing' and alters the calculation order to *before* processing fees. The contradicted text contains both 'profit sharing' and the calculation *before* processing fees. The original text had a different calculation order of after the fees, so with the change of terms, now the calculation order also changes, and causes a contradiction between the original text, and the changed text. This affects the amount USA MCO receives and creates uncertainty about the actual compensation, directly conflicting with the terms and calculations laid out. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where multiple terms are used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least sixty 90 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least ninety 60 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'sixty 90 days prior'. The changed text inverts it to 'ninety 60 days prior'. This creates a clear contradiction regarding the notification period required to terminate the agreement, leading to uncertainty about when a party can effectively terminate. This contradition will impact the time required of termination within the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least ninety 60 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue for a extended period of five years from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.justification: The modified text alters the notification period for termination from 'sixty 90 days' to 'ninety 60 days'. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which also specifies the notification period as 'ninety 60 days'. This creates a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency within the agreement regarding the time required for termination notice, making it ambiguous and therefore unenforceable. Because the definition of termination is ambiguous now between 60 and 90 days, and this definition is changed within the same sentence, it causes in-text contradiction as specified by the prompt. Specifically, this falls under the Inconsistencies definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that all publicity and public announcements concerning the formation and existence of this Agreement shall be jointly planned and coordinated by and among the Parties. Neither party shall disclose any of the specific terms of this Agreement to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be withheld unreasonably.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that all publicity and public announcements concerning the formation and existence of this Agreement shall be jointly planned and coordinated by and among the Parties. Either party *may* disclose any of the specific terms of this Agreement to any third party *with* the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be withheld unreasonably.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Neither party shall disclose...without the prior written consent" to "Either party may disclose...with the prior written consent" subtly alters the obligation. This replaces a prohibition with permission, creating uncertainty and contradiction. The original text implies confidentiality unless consent is given, while the changed text suggests parties can disclose the agreement if they have consent. The contradiction lies in whether disclosing the terms is generally prohibited (original) or conditionally allowed (changed).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that all publicity and public announcements concerning the formation and existence of this Agreement shall be jointly planned and coordinated by and among the Parties. Either party *may* disclose any of the specific terms of this Agreement to any third party *with* the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be withheld unreasonably. Notwithstanding the foregoing, any party may disclose information concerning this Agreement as required by the rules, orders, regulations, subpoenas or directives of a court, government or governmental agency, after giving prior notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text is the same as the contradicted text. There is not a strong contradiction. While the original text stated that 'Neither party shall disclose,' it was modified to 'Either party may disclose...with the prior written consent'. The contradicted text which is the Changed Text includes a clause stating 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, any party may disclose information concerning this Agreement as required by the rules...'. The 'Notwithstanding the foregoing' clause serves as an exception to the general rule, therefore, no contradiction exists.
